title: Capstone Scope, Dependency Graph, Milestones, and Risk Control · Decision Evidence Lens: Critical Path Buffer
summary: The plan is organized by components and deadlines rather than prerequisite evidence, risk retirement, end-to-end value, and independent assessment gates. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone critical path buffer increment in evidence-ordered capstone delivery system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers.
Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Critical Path Buffer · Theory Critical Path Buffer
steps.instruction:
theory case — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Critical Path Buffer contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Critical Path Buffer · Theory Critical Path Buffer.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers. For Predict Critical Path Buffer · Theory Critical Path Buffer, keep Critical Path Buffer connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The worked example variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers. A defensible solution applies Critical Path Buffer, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to evidence-ordered capstone delivery system.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Adding more parallel work always shortens the critical path. This fails the Critical Path Buffer contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: SPDX specification (SPDX 3.0.1) — Controls machine-readable software, package, file, snippet, licensing, security, build, and AI artifact relationships.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers.

stimulus.title: Critical Path Buffer failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Adding more parallel work always shortens the critical path.

lines.label: required

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Critical Path Buffer, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the critical path buffer defect evidence boundary.
